title: carya ovata var. borealis
intro: Carya ovata var. borealis, also known as the northern shellbark hickory, is a variety of deciduous tree native to eastern North America. It is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant compounds, that have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions. The extracts from this tree have been reported to have potential health benefits such as lowering cholesterol levels, improving cardiovascular health, and reducing the risk of certain diseases. Northern shellbark hickory is often used in herbal preparations, including teas, tinctures, and salves, and can be found in traditional medicine and health supplements.
